title: Exploring five types of beam shaping using tiled-aperture coherent beam combining
claim_supported: Demonstrates five distinct beam-shaping outcomes achievable via tiled-aperture coherent beam combining (CBC), a scalable architecture for high-power laser power/beam-quality scaling relevant to industrial and directed-energy laser subsystems.

title: 260 fs, 403 W coherently combined fiber laser with precise high-order dispersion management
claim_supported: A two-channel coherently combined ultrafast fiber laser achieves 403 W average power at 260 fs pulse duration (504 microJ pulse energy) via precise high-order (up to 4th-order) dispersion compensation, reducing pulse duration from 488 fs to 260 fs and raising primary-peak energy fraction from 65% to 92%; beam quality M^2 < 1.2.
locator: Springer/Frontiers of Optoelectronics article page, January 2024 issue
notes: DOI confirmed via Springer/journal.hep.com.cn/PubMed cross-referenced URLs all resolving to the same 10.1007/s12200-024-00107-5. PMC10803720 and PubMed 38252379 both corroborate existence and date. Article full text not fetched this session (search-derived summary only).

title: India DRDO Mk-II(A) 30 kW Laser Directed Energy Weapon successful trial
authors_or_org: Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO), India
claim_supported: On April 13, 2025, DRDO announced the successful full-scale trial of its Mk-II(A) laser directed-energy weapon (30 kW, ~5 km range against drones/fixed-wing aircraft/swarms, radar+EO detection, integrated electronic-warfare jamming) at the National Open Air Range, Kurnool, Andhra Pradesh; DRDO is separately developing a 300 kW, 20 km-range 'Surya' system with comprehensive trials anticipated by mid-2026.
locator: armyrecognition.com article, April 2025, citing DRDO's official announcement
notes: The official DRDO/PIB source document (drdo.gov.in/drdo/sites/default/files/drdo-news-documents/NPC12to15Apr2025.pdf) returned HTTP 404 on direct fetch this session; this record instead cites a defense-trade-press report of the official DRDO announcement, tiered T2 pending verifier location of the primary PIB/DRDO release.

title: A*STAR and GlobalFoundries Master Research Collaboration Agreement on silicon photonics
authors_or_org: Agency for Science, Technology and Research (A*STAR), Singapore; GlobalFoundries
claim_supported: In November 2025, A*STAR and GlobalFoundries signed a Master Research Collaboration Agreement to advance silicon photonics in Singapore, including a planned GF silicon-photonics R&D Center of Excellence focused on next-generation 400 Gbps data-transfer materials; separately, A*STAR's Institute of Microelectronics announced a late-2025 upgrade of its 12-inch silicon-photonics pilot line to support advanced 3D integration for global chipmaker prototyping.
locator: A*STAR press release, November 2025 (per WebSearch synthesis)
notes: Direct WebFetch of the A*STAR press-release URL returned HTTP 404 this session; figures/claims per WebSearch synthesis of A*STAR's and GlobalFoundries' own announcement language only. Tiered T2 pending verifier direct fetch. No investment figure was disclosed in the available reporting.

title: GAO-23-105868: Directed Energy Weapons: DOD Should Focus on Transition Planning
claim_supported: GAO report (published April 17, 2023): DOD allocates approximately $1 billion annually to directed-energy-weapon development; DOD and military departments have developed multiple high-energy-laser and high-power-microwave demonstrators/prototypes that have successfully shot down drones in live-fire demonstrations, but face a persistent 'valley of death' transitioning prototypes to acquisition programs; GAO made four recommendations (Navy and Air Force to establish documented transition agreements and user-feedback processes); as of August 2025 all four recommendations remained open.
locator: GAO-23-105868, full report and highlights page
notes: Directly fetched and confirmed this session. Establishes the ~$1B/year DoD directed-energy budget baseline that contextualizes the JLWS awards (L12-031/032) and DARPA programs (L12-040/041) in this ledger.

title: GAO-25-107204: National Nuclear Security Administration: Improvements Needed for Managing Recapitalization of Fusion Facilities
claim_supported: GAO report on NNSA's management of fusion-facility (including NIF laser-system) recapitalization, relevant to the EYC laser-upgrade program's oversight and funding pathway (L12-042).
locator: GAO-25-107204, report landing page
notes: Title and existence confirmed via search result only (gao.gov domain); not directly fetched this session. Verifier should fetch to extract specific findings/recommendations.

title: DARPA Persistent Optical Wireless Energy Relay (POWER) program sets distance/power record
claim_supported: In May 2025, DARPA's POWER program demonstrated more than 800 W of optical power delivered over 8.6 km (5.3 miles) during a 30-second transmission, with over 20% optical-to-electrical conversion efficiency at shorter range, in partnership with Teravec Technologies, Packet Digital, RIT, the Naval Research Laboratory, and HELSTF (White Sands Missile Range).
locator: darpa.mil news release, May 2025
notes: Directly fetched and confirmed this session. Directed laser power-beaming is an adjacent beam-control/beam-delivery subsystem application within L12 scope (not a weapon program but a power-relay program using the same high-power laser/beam-control building blocks).

title: IEC 60825 series: Safety of laser products (2025 SER edition)
claim_supported: IEC 60825 is the internationally adopted laser-product-safety standard series (equipment classification 180 nm-1 mm wavelength range); a consolidated 2025 SER package was released January 20, 2025 including updated parts (e.g., IEC TS 60825-19:2024, IEC TS 60825-20:2025); IEC 60825-1 Ed.3.0 (220 pages, 2014 edition still current base document) is priced at $665.00 via the Laser Institute of America store.
locator: IEC/en-standard.eu package listing; Laser Institute of America (LIA) store pricing page
notes: Not directly fetched this session (iec.ch webstore pages did not display price in search results; price confirmed via LIA store search snippet). This is the governing safety standard underlying every industrial/medical/defense laser product referenced elsewhere in this ledger.

title: ISO 11553-1:2020 -- Safety of machinery -- Laser processing machines -- Part 1: Laser safety requirements
claim_supported: ISO 11553-1:2020 specifies laser-radiation-hazard identification and safety requirements for laser processing machines (the industrial machine-tool class covering cutting/welding/marking laser systems), superseding ISO 11553-1:2005 and the original ISO 11553:1996.
locator: iso.org standard 67658 landing page
notes: Direct fetch of iso.org returned HTTP 403 this session; scope/edition history confirmed via search-result titles across iso.org, cen (iteh.ai), and BSI listings. Price not confirmed this session.

title: ISO 11553-2:2007 -- Safety of machinery -- Laser processing machines -- Part 2: Safety requirements for hand-held or hand-operated laser processing devices
claim_supported: ISO 11553-2:2007 specifies safety requirements for hand-held or hand-operated laser processing machines (e.g., handheld laser welding/cleaning tools), a distinct requirement set from fixed laser processing machines (Part 1), relevant to the fast-growing handheld-laser-welding export segment noted in Raycus's 2025 results (L12-038).
locator: iso.org standard 37590 landing page
notes: Not directly fetched this session (search-result title/scope only).

title: IPG Photonics YLS Series high-power CW fiber laser product line
claim_supported: IPG's YLS product family spans 500 W to 100+ kW CW output, electrical wall-plug efficiency >40% (YLS-ECO variant >50%), full 10%-100% dynamic power range with no beam-quality change, and multi-year (>10-year) field-operating lifetimes -- the reference industrial high-power fiber laser platform against which Chinese competitors (Raycus, Han's Laser, Maxphotonics) are explicitly benchmarked in IPG's own 10-K risk disclosure (L12-034).
locator: ipgphotonics.com YLS 1-120 kW product page
notes: Not directly fetched this session (WebSearch synthesis of product-page content only). No unit price disclosed; IPG, like other laser OEMs in this lane, does not publish list prices openly -- pricing is opaque and would require direct RFQ, consistent with the pattern also observed in the L06 lane for RF-power subsystem vendors.

title: Thorlabs Adaptive Optics product line (MEMS and piezoelectric deformable mirrors, wavefront sensors, AO kits)
claim_supported: Thorlabs offers packaged adaptive-optics kits (deformable mirror + Shack-Hartmann wavefront sensor + control software + optomechanics), including MEMS-based deformable mirrors via a Boston Micromachines Corporation partnership and its own piezoelectric (biomorph-bender) DMP40-series mirrors -- representative of the commercial off-the-shelf adaptive-optics component base available to integrators building beam-control subsystems for industrial or directed-energy laser systems (contrasted with custom high-power AO systems like Apollon's, L12-011).
locator: thorlabs.com Adaptive Optics landing page and MEMS/piezoelectric deformable-mirror product pages
notes: Not directly fetched this session (WebSearch synthesis only). No price disclosed in available search results.

title: Gigaphoton DUV/EUV excimer laser light source product lineup
claim_supported: Gigaphoton (a Komatsu Group company) has shipped nearly 2,000 excimer lasers for semiconductor lithography worldwide and continues to develop both DUV (ArF immersion) and EUV drive-laser/light-source technology; at SPIE Advanced Lithography + Patterning 2025 (San Jose, Feb 2025) it presented on next-generation ArF immersion light sources, DUV light-source availability improvements, and inspection-EUV/advanced-packaging-DUV light sources -- Japan's principal domestic alternative/complement to ASML/Cymer in the lithography light-source supply chain.
locator: gigaphoton.com/ja product and news pages (Japanese-language)
notes: Located via a Japanese-language query; not directly fetched this session (WebSearch synthesis of Japanese-language page content only). No pricing disclosed. This is the strongest Japan-specific primary vendor evidence found in this lane this session; a follow-up scout could usefully fetch Gigaphoton's SPIE 2025 papers directly for technical detail.

title: China fiber laser market size and localization-penetration data (Huajing Industry Research Institute, via Sina Finance)
authors_or_org: Huajing Industry Research Institute (华经产业研究院), reported by Sina Finance (新浪财经)
claim_supported: China's fiber-laser market grew from RMB 7.74 billion (2018) to RMB 12.26 billion (2022), reported as a 9.6% CAGR (self-consistency check on the stated endpoints instead implies roughly a 12% CAGR -- flagged as an internal inconsistency in the source, not independently reconciled); domestic high-power fiber-laser localization penetration rose from 6.56% (2016) to 76.19% (2021), forecast to 90.03% (2022); mid-power localization ~59%, low-power 99.78%; as of year-end 2022 Raycus held 26.8% domestic market share, overtaking IPG domestically for the first time, with the top-3 (Raycus/IPG/Chuangxin-Maxphotonics) holding CR3 >70%.
locator: Sina Finance article, September 9, 2025, citing Huajing Industry Research Institute data
notes: Directly fetched and confirmed this session (Chinese-language). NOTE: the underlying market-share/localization figures cited in this September-2025-published article are dated to 2021-2022, several years stale relative to the publication date -- flagged for verifier; do not treat as current-year (2025) market share without confirming a more recent breakdown. Also flagged: the article's stated 9.6% CAGR does not match the compound growth rate implied by its own stated 2018/2022 endpoints (~12%) -- an unreconciled internal inconsistency in the source, reported here rather than silently corrected.
